comment.body:
i always said to my classmates, bcs some of them always said ,that "oh why do i need to know history, chemistry, math, old poems, etc."
and i said... "look, first this is the heritage of our 'species', when you have a basic understand about the world around you is not just respect towards your ancessors, but also makes you a bit more independent. comment.body: I used to be an equities market maker.  It is the only job I have enjoyed.  When they went penny spread, it killed the business where I was.  One thing trading does is keep you abreast of the world and news.  I'm not a news junkie, but I do like that aspect besides the challenge.  And market makers have a LOT of advantages.  You see the flow, literally, and you can use limit orders to 'trade against' - kind of like stops

comment.body:
oh, nice... i guessed you are a very experienced trader, based on your inputs, i told you few days ago, to share more of that. we like them.
(and finally, you understand the greek + vol etc. inputs i post)

trading is very similar to chess in that terms that it reveals the weaknesses and strenghts of the spirit. 
one must be trained very hard mentally to bear it. 
it is a constant self-developping, and puzzle solving, and this is why i enjoyed + it pays off well. 

MMs edge... i agree only, bcs many traders have no knowledge about those mechanisms. but flows can change very fast sometimes... and they are always tailored to the actual structures of the momentum. i think, thats were the first step towards the edge lies
